source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane - Power Loss

actions_taken: Crew members dispatched to communicate power loss to 175 persons onboard
UPS-3451 bypassed to restore power to Fire and Gas processor
Nolan Power Technician replaced capacitor bank on UPS-3451
BSEE and USCG joint investigation conducted
event.summary: On 6 May 2019 at 0748 hours, Shell Offshore Inc. MC 437-A (Appomattox) platform experienced a partial loss of primary generator power during a fuel swap operation from diesel to natural gas. The Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS-3451) failed to provide backup power due to shorted capacitors and undersized fuses on the inverter capacitor bank. This caused shutdown of the fire and gas processor, preventing emergency generator startup. The UPS was bypassed and power restored. A second power loss occurred at 2147 hours but the emergency generator restored power within 30 seconds with the UPS in bypass. Root cause was identified as turbine generator trip combined with UPS-3451 failure. No injuries or pollution resulted. The facility had not commenced first oil production at the time of the incident.

threats.name: Primary Generator Power Loss During Fuel Swap

consequences.name: Loss of Emergency Power Generation Capability
Loss of Fire and Gas Detection System
Loss of Facility Alarms and Communication Systems
Potential Safety Risk to 175 Personnel Onboard

controls.name: Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS-3451)
controls.barrier_role: Backup power supply to maintain critical systems during primary power loss
controls.lod_basis: Secondary power system to prevent loss of critical safety functions

supporting_text: the Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS) failed to supply backup power
Crew members found that UPS-3451 malfunctioned
The UPS system failed due to shorted capacitors and undersized fuses on the inverter capacitor bank

controls.name: Emergency Generator
controls.barrier_role: Restore power to critical systems following primary power loss
controls.lod_basis: Secondary power generation system to restore facility operations

supporting_text: The UPS failure caused a shutdown of the fire and gas processor, which prevented the emergency generator from starting
They bypassed UPS-3451 and restored power to the Fire and Gas processor, which allowed the emergency generator to start
with UPS-3451 in bypass, the emergency generator restored power within 30 seconds

controls.name: Fire and Gas Processor
controls.barrier_role: Enable emergency generator startup and maintain fire/gas detection capability
controls.lod_basis: Critical system required for emergency power generation and safety monitoring

supporting_text: The UPS failure caused a shutdown of the fire and gas processor, which prevented the emergency generator from starting
They bypassed UPS-3451 and restored power to the Fire and Gas processor, which allowed the emergency generator to start

controls.name: Offshore Installation Manager (OIM) Safety Stand Down
controls.barrier_role: Communicate power loss and coordinate safe response to all personnel onboard
controls.lod_basis: Management response to mitigate consequences of power loss to personnel safety

supporting_text: The Offshore Installation Manager (OIM) initiated a safety stand down
He dispatched crew members throughout the facility to communicate the power loss to the 175 persons onboard

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown (ESD) System
controls.barrier_role: Ensure safe shutdown of subsea wells and safety valves if required
controls.lod_basis: Final safety system to prevent uncontrolled release of hydrocarbons

supporting_text: the BSEE investigator verified that the Emergency Shutdown (ESD), all the subsea wells, Surface Controlled Subsurface Safety Valves (SCSSVs), Shutdown Valves (SDVs), and Boarding Shutdown Valves (BSDVs) would have functioned as per the approved Deepwater Operation Plan (DWOP)

controls.name: Subsea Safety Valves (SCSSVs, SDVs, BSDVs)
controls.barrier_role: Prevent uncontrolled hydrocarbon release from subsea wells
controls.lod_basis: Final containment barrier for subsea production systems

supporting_text: the BSEE investigator verified that the Emergency Shutdown (ESD), all the subsea wells, Surface Controlled Subsurface Safety Valves (SCSSVs), Shutdown Valves (SDVs), and Boarding Shutdown Valves (BSDVs) would have functioned as per the approved Deepwater Operation Plan (DWOP)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
